title: alnus viridis subsp. brembana
intro: Alnus viridis subsp. brembana, also known as the Italian green alder, is a subspecies of the green alder tree (Alnus viridis) native to the Italian Alps and surrounding regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Alnus viridis subsp. brembana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate congestion, and soothe sore throats. Herbal preparations of this plant typically include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, syrups, and topical applications for various health issues.
